text: Alex, you always say that your capacity
to work itself is trainable. Yes. How do
you make sure you don't affect your
health? For example, in book launch
days, you were not sleeping much. Yeah,
but I'm still alive, right?
I mean, I'm sorry.
>> [laughter]
>> Like, bro, I want to win.
I mean, like
Like, you're not going to die. You'll be
okay.
Like, if you're working so hard that
you're like not sleeping as much, fine.
Now, I'm not saying you shouldn't sleep.
You absolutely should sleep. But
sometimes sometimes it's wartime, right?
And I think um
this term keeps coming up in my mind and
maybe I'll write a book about it cuz I
love the term so much, but like
built for war,
right? Like
I remember like like during the launch,
I just thought to myself, I was like
I am built for this.
And some people aren't and I think
that's fine. Like Like, winning isn't
for everyone. That's why only a few
people can do it. And so like we have to
be willing to make trade-offs. Like
there are no solutions, there are only
trade-offs. You trade one thing for
another and then you decide whether it's
worth it or not. And literally the
entirety of life is making those trades.
That's it. And some people trade
short-term for, you know, short trade
long-term for short-term. Some people
trade getting high for being productive.
Some people trade Netflix for getting
forward in life. And those are just
trades. Nothing wrong with it. It's only
wrong if it's not getting what you want,
right? And so like the health thing,
yeah, be healthy. Long-term you get I
mean, obviously your longevity in the
game is going to be important, but I
would struggle to say like show me
somebody who's ultra successful who
hasn't had periods or seasons where
they're lopsided. And so my
encouragement for you during these like
seasons of lots of work is to think
about work-life balance on a longer time
horizon. And so meaning like you might
have the decade of your 20s being a work
season and the decade of your 30s being
a semi-work season with more family. And
then as you kind of progress on, those
seasons will shift.
But like if you're not where you want to
be, you will have to do more stuff to
get there.
And until you have money,
you will basically have to use work as
your primary mode of getting ahead. And
so you have to use violent,
unreasonable, ruthless amounts of effort
for extended periods of time and be able
to delay gratification because you sure
as hell aren't going to win fast. And so
if you need that to keep going, good
luck. But the vast majority of people
have something that they're running
towards or something they're running
away from. And whatever it is that fuels
you, use it. If you're business owner
and you are not growing as fast as you'd
like, I'd like to give you a free gift.
So my team and I put together the $100
million scaling road map, which is
basically 200 hours of us looking over
all the portfolio companies we've had
and what stages of growth they went
through and more importantly where they
got stuck and how they got past it. And
so we broke it in these 10 stages and we
made this little kind of quiz thing
where if you put in your business
information, it'll tell you where you're
at and the most important part for you,
what to do for each of the functions of
the business across product, marketing,
sales, customer success, recruiting, IT,
human resources and finance. And so no
matter what you're struggling with,
someone else has already struggled with
it and solved it. And so I'd like to
give you this thing absolutely free. You
can go to acquisition.com/roadmap,
plug in your business information and if
you want us to actually help you
deconstruct the business and you're
trying to scale, we'd love to help you
out. On the thank you page, you can book
a call with my team and we will look at
the business, see if we can help and if
we can, we'll invite you out to Vegas
and we'll do this in person live.
